headline: Manning's Arm Overwhelms Washington in Dominant 47-21 Texas Victory
body: Arch Manning put on a clinic Saturday, threading passes with precision and anticipating coverage windows that left Washington's secondary scrambling. His four touchdown passes and 285 yards on efficient 20-of-30 accuracy established Texas's offensive identity early and never allowed the visiting Huskies a foothold. Texas built its lead methodically in the first half, converting short fields into scoring drives while forcing Washington into reactive football.

The turning point arrived in the second quarter when Manning hit Anderson for a touchdown on a perfectly placed third-and-seven throw down the sideline. That score pushed Texas to 24-7 and signaled to Washington that they were facing a quarterback executing at a level their defense could not match. From that point forward, the Huskies were playing catchup against an offense that had found its rhythm, and quarterback Williams Jr.'s efforts—two touchdowns of his own—became consolation rather than a genuine threat.

Texas's defense wasn't flashy but was sufficiently stout, with Biles recording a sack and anchoring the front. Washington managed 244 passing yards and 87 rushing yards, numbers that reflect an offense never quite able to establish dominance. The 26-point margin understates nothing: this was Texas asserting itself as a team playing a different speed than its opponent. Manning's control of the line of scrimmage and field position proved decisive.
turningPoint: Anderson's sideline touchdown reception in the second quarter stretched Texas's lead to 24-7 and established Manning's efficiency as uncontainable.

unsungHero.name: Anderson
unsungHero.why: Seven receptions for 100 yards without a touchdown often gets overlooked, but Anderson's consistent separation and route-running gave Manning easy completions that sustained drives when Washington needed stops most.
